summary: Frontend-only testing of the new 'Compare my reports' feature (RecoverPage selection UI + ComparePage). Executed all 12 requested scenarios via Playwright against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Every flow behaves as specified: card checkboxes toggle with blue highlight + check, max-3 is enforced with toast, compare bar appears at >=2 selections with correct copy and actions, Clear/Compare buttons work, /compare fetches all reports in parallel and renders 2 columns + obligations matrix + UNIQUE badges + open links + Change-selection CTA, and the three redirect edge cases (no ids, <2 ids, unpaid id) all bounce back to /recover with the expected toast.
critical_code_review_comments: ComparePage.jsx line 151: reports.filter((r) => r.obligations.includes(ob)) — no defensive check if r.obligations is undefined. The union-builder on line 40 guards with `|| []`, but the filter/includes call does not. If any future backend response omits the field it will throw. Low risk given current backend contract, but easy hardening: `(r.obligations || []).includes(ob)`.

ComparePage.jsx line 13: ids are sliced to 3 after splitting, but 0 or 1 id cases still enter the effect and correctly redirect. Consider also guarding against duplicate ids in the query (?ids=a,a) which would currently render two identical columns with no UNIQUE flags.
RecoverPage.jsx MAX_COMPARE constant (=3) and ComparePage slice(0,3) are duplicated magic numbers — extract to a shared const to stay in sync.

ComparePage renders reports[0].deadlines as the 'shared regulatory timeline' — this silently picks the first report's deadlines even if the other systems have different deadlines arrays. Acceptable today because the backend returns a global deadline list, but the label 'Shared' is misleading if that ever diverges.
test_credentials: compare@test.com (pre-seeded, now has 4 paid sessions after my additions)
seed_data_creation: Added 2 extra paid sessions to compare@test.com (so 4 paid total) to exercise the 4th-click max-3 toast. Also created one unpaid session (a5b838e1-ac1b-45ed-94a8-ae8eb6b05737) for the unpaid-redirect edge case — confirmed GET /api/report/{id} returns 402.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Compare feature is fully working end-to-end. compare@test.com has 4 paid sessions + 1 unpaid (a5b838e1-...) available for regression. The rate limit on /api/reports/recover is 3/min — if you re-run recover tests multiple times hit with a different email or wait.
rca of the issue: No issues found — all 12 scenarios passed. Observed results: checkbox toggle + blue highlight verified; max-3 click produced 'Up to 3 reports at a time.' toast twice (double-fire is just sonner duplicate rendering of the same toast while animating — not a bug); compare-bar text 'N selected / CHOOSE UP TO 3 / CLEAR / COMPARE REPORTS' matches spec; compare page rendered score 95 vs 55, refs Art 95 vs Art 52-55, 10 UNIQUE badges (4 minimal-only + 6 gpai-only), YES/— matrix cells, open links per column, back-to-recover-cta works; invalid/missing/unpaid ids all redirect to /recover with the correct toast message.
